contradiction_text: Larger retrieved contexts improve recall relative to oracle files, but they still reduce model performance because models struggle to localize the relevant code within the added context.
related_claim_texts: Retrieval quality appears to be a major bottleneck in SWE-bench, because oracle-based retrieval improves Claude 2 performance over BM25 even though larger retrieved contexts can still hurt performance.
disclosure: Keep visible that "more context" is not unambiguously beneficial here: retrieval recall improves, but end-to-end task resolution can still worsen because localization remains a separate bottleneck.

contradiction_text: Oracle retrieval materially improves performance over BM25, which supports retrieval as a major bottleneck, but the oracle setting is less realistic than the benchmark’s main evaluation regime and therefore does not show that practical retrieval improvements alone would close the gap.
related_claim_texts: Under the benchmark's main BM25 retrieval setting, the evaluated models performed very poorly, with the best reported result solving only about 2% of tasks.

Retrieval quality appears to be a major bottleneck in SWE-bench, because oracle-based retrieval improves Claude 2 performance over BM25 even though larger retrieved contexts can still hurt performance.
disclosure: Keep visible that the benchmark clearly shows poor performance under BM25 and meaningful improvement under oracle retrieval, but those oracle gains are not direct evidence that realistic retrieval alone would produce comparable results.

contradiction_text: SWE-bench is grounded in real software-maintenance episodes, but its admission rules are highly selective, so its realism coexists with a narrower task class than general software engineering.
related_claim_texts: SWE-bench is built from real software maintenance episodes but narrows a much larger pull-request pool into 2,294 executable benchmark tasks across 12 Python repositories.
SWE-bench task admission is intentionally strict, requiring merged issue-resolving pull requests with test-file changes, at least one fail-to-pass test, and no installation or runtime errors.
disclosure: Keep visible that the benchmark’s use of real repository history supports ecological grounding, but the strict filtering means it represents a constrained executable slice rather than the full space of software work.
